name: John Lear
description: John Lear (1942-2020) was an American conspiracy theorist and pilot who gained notoriety for his claims regarding extraterrestrial life, government cover-ups, and secret bases on the Moon and Mars. Lear asserted that the Moon had an atmosphere, vegetation, and a human population. He also claimed the existence of a secret government organization, Majestic 12 (MJ-12), dedicated to managing information about extraterrestrial encounters. His theories often involved alien abduction, genetic manipulation by aliens, and a shadow government controlling world events. Lear's claims gained traction within UFO and conspiracy communities, contributing to the spread of various alternative narratives. Mainstream science and historical analysis dismiss Lear's claims as lacking credible evidence and often based on misinterpretations or fabrications. Skeptics point to the lack of verifiable proof for his extraordinary assertions and highlight inconsistencies within his narratives. Despite the lack of scientific support, Lear's theories remain popular in certain circles, contributing to the ongoing fascination with UFOs, government secrecy, and alternative explanations for unexplained phenomena.
subtitle: Pilot and UFO Conspiracy Theorist

claims.claim: The Moon has an atmosphere, vegetation, and a human population.
claims.evidence: Alleged NASA photographs and whistleblower testimonies.
claims.criticism: NASA denies these claims. No independent confirmation exists. Scientific observations contradict the presence of a substantial atmosphere or vegetation.

claims.claim: The existence of Majestic 12 (MJ-12), a secret government organization managing extraterrestrial information.
claims.evidence: The 'MJ-12 documents,' purported leaked documents detailing the organization's existence.
claims.criticism: The MJ-12 documents have been widely discredited as forgeries by historians, document experts, and the FBI. They contain numerous inconsistencies and anachronisms.

claims.claim: Aliens are involved in genetic manipulation of humans.
claims.evidence: Anecdotal accounts of alien abductions and alleged genetic similarities between humans and certain alien species.
claims.criticism: Alien abduction accounts are often attributed to sleep paralysis, suggestibility, and psychological factors. Genetic similarities are explained by evolutionary biology and do not require extraterrestrial intervention.

keyFigures.name: John Lear
keyFigures.role: Main proponent and popularizer of the theories

keyFigures.name: Stanton Friedman
keyFigures.role: Ufologist who initially supported the MJ-12 documents, later expressed doubts

keyFigures.name: Kal Korff
keyFigures.role: Skeptic and debunker of UFO claims, including Lear's theories

timeline.event: John Lear begins publicly promoting his UFO and conspiracy theories.
Lear testifies before the Citizens' Hearing on Disclosure.

sources.author: Curtis Peebles
sources.work: Watch the Skies!: A Chronicle of the Flying Saucer Myth
sources.title: Curtis Peebles
